diagnosis: Agent converges to a safe hover that never lands because the per-step rewards (progress, efficiency) are too weak to overcome the constant termination penalty, and the bonus for reaching the pad is too small and threshold too tight.

proposed_changes.reason: Doubles the penalty for being far from the pad and expands the close-range reward zone, strongly incentivizing the agent to reduce pad_distance.
Reduces the fuel penalty by a factor of 3, allowing the agent to use thrusters more freely for maneuvering toward the pad without incurring large negative rewards.

Increases the termination penalty from -5 to -15 to make the constant negative outcome more salient, pushing the agent to avoid truncation by landing successfully. Combined with increased progress incentives, this creates a stronger drive to complete the landing.

predicted_effect: The agent will start moving horizontally toward the pad, descend more aggressively, and eventually attempt landing. The larger termination penalty and stronger progress gradient should break the safe-hover local optimum.
max_risk: The agent may crash more frequently (increased fall_rate) if it descends too fast or tilts excessively while maneuvering, leading to high-speed ground contact.
risk_mitigation: Monitor fall_rate and leg_contact metrics closely. If fall_rate rises above 0.1, reduce the progress coefficient or increase the efficiency penalty to encourage smoother approaches. Also track vertical_speed_magnitude; if it exceeds 1.5, consider a velocity penalty.
